page: Microsoft Azure E64-32ds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the EDSv5 family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It is equipped with 2458 GB of local NVMe storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates balanced performance across compute and memory subsystems. The server achieved a Passmark memory score of 2902.8, placing it in the top 25% tier, which aligns with its memory-optimized design. Large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks show read speeds of 149.6 GB/sec and write speeds of 66.85 GB/sec. In compute tasks, the Intel Xeon processor delivered a Geekbench multi-core score of 14288.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 31084.36. Image processing performance is a notable highlight, scoring 14009.0 in Geekbench, which is classified in the top 25% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E64-32ds_v5 offers high memory density and substantial bundled local storage, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads without requiring external storage attachments for temporary data. However, there are clear performance tradeoffs. The lack of dedicated GPU hardware limits its utility for heavy parallel computing, as evidenced by a weak prompt processing speed of 6.43 tokens/sec on a large 70B LLM model and unavailable text generation. Consequently, this server is best suited for large relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, enterprise applications, and data analytics pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of RAM and 2458 GB of local storage. The server delivers strong memory performance, highlighted by a Passmark memory score in the top 25% tier and a large-block read bandwidth of 149.6 GB/sec. While its compute capabilities are solid, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 14288.0, the absence of a GPU results in weak performance for large-scale AI workloads, such as 70B LLM prompt processing. This configuration is highly cost-efficient for memory-dense applications, making it an appropriate choice for large databases, in-memory caching, and data-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ds_v5 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, designed for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ds_v5 is a memory-optimized server with 32 Intel vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high RAM density and substantial local storage for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8370C processors at 2.8 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB per core
2458 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Best suited for large databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
Limited suitability for large LLM inference due to the lack of GPU acceleration
